steps.title: Identify the parenthetical structure
steps.content: An em dash after “NATO” begins a parenthetical list of example member nations and their joining dates. The parenthetical must be closed before the main clause continues.

steps.title: Apply the paired em dash rule
steps.content:
When a parenthetical element opens with an em dash, it must close with an em dash to properly separate it from the main clause.

Choice D provides the em dash after 2004 to close the parenthetical.

Choices A, B, and C fail to close it by using incorrect punctuation (semicolon, comma, or none).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
